patterns.why: Panoramic rail tickets do not replace the mandatory seat reservation, and popular departures can sell out.
patterns.deadline_rule: reserve weeks ahead for peak dates
patterns.backup: Use regional rail legs on the same corridor or shift to a less constrained departure.

patterns.why: Panoramic-car seats require a separate reservation from the rail ticket.
patterns.deadline_rule: reserve weeks ahead for peak dates
patterns.backup: Use regional trains over the Bernina route if panoramic seats are unavailable.

patterns.why: International high-speed rail has capacity controls and better fares before close-in dates.
patterns.deadline_rule: book as soon as the travel date is firm
patterns.backup: Keep a flight or ferry routing as a timing fallback.

patterns.why: Cross-border high-speed seats can be capacity-limited and sell through before local legs.
patterns.deadline_rule: book once route and date are stable
patterns.backup: Break the route into domestic legs or compare a short-haul flight.

patterns.why: Classic Inca Trail access is permit-limited and operator-managed; there is no ordinary late release to wait for.
patterns.deadline_rule: secure with an authorized operator months ahead
patterns.backup: Use an alternate Sacred Valley trek or a non-permit Machu Picchu visit plan.

patterns.why: Nasrid Palaces entry is timed and often sells out separately from broader Granada planning.
patterns.deadline_rule: reserve a timed palace slot weeks ahead
patterns.backup: Hold a Granada day with Generalife/Alcazaba focus or use a reputable guided slot if available.

patterns.why: Small timed-entry inventory makes same-week availability unreliable.
patterns.backup: Use a Milan art itinerary that does not depend on Cenacolo entry.

patterns.why: Cable access requires a permit lottery rather than a normal admission ticket.
patterns.deadline_rule: enter the seasonal or daily lottery before committing the hike day
patterns.backup: Plan a Yosemite hike day around Clouds Rest, Mist Trail, or Glacier Point instead.
